title: Contributing guidelines
text: document outlines propose change otargen package.

title: Contributing guidelines
text: recommend create Git branch pull request (PR). use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat. Contributions test cases included easier accept. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md current development version header describing changes made followed GitHub username, links relevant issue(s)/PR(s).

title: Contributing guidelines
text: make substantial pull request, always file issue make sure someone developer team agrees ’s problem. ’ve found bug, create associated issue illustrate bug.

title: Contributing guidelines
text: contributors maintainers project, pledge respect people contribute reporting issues, posting feature requests, updating documentation, submitting pull requests patches, activities. committed making participation project harassment-free experience everyone, regardless level experience, gender, gender identity expression, sexual orientation, disability, personal appearance, body size, race, ethnicity, age, religion. Examples unacceptable behavior participants include use sexual language imagery, derogatory comments personal attacks, trolling, public private harassment, insults, unprofessional conduct. Project maintainers right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct. Project maintainers follow Code Conduct may removed project team. Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported opening issue contacting one project maintainers. Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant (https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/)

title: Examples
text: Objective: 🌌 Traverse genomic landscape getLociGenes(). Chart composition genes within specified chromosomal region obtain detailed information gene tidy dataframe. Function: getLociGenes() chromosome, start, end: geographic coordinates genomic exploration.

title: Examples
text: Objective: 🚀 Navigate genetic cosmos studiesAndLeadVariantsForGeneByL2G(). L2G model core development Open Target Genetics platform star map identify potential causal genes across GWAS universe. Function: studiesAndLeadVariantsForGeneByL2G() Parameters: gene, l2g, pvalue, vtype: parameters guide exploration. Open Target Genetics portal Interpreting L2G score L2G model produces score, ranging 0 1, reflects approximate fraction GSP genes among genes near given threshold. can interpreted say genes L2G score 0.5 50% chance causal gene locus, assumption model correct locus similar gold-standard training data. Note: models don’t generalize perfectly, true fraction causal genes L2G score 0.5 likely less 50%. key strength L2G score aggregates information credible set variants, rather looking distance lead GWAS variant genes.
code: # Retrieve L2G results for the gene PCSK9 with specified filters l2g_results <- studiesAndLeadVariantsForGeneByL2G(   gene = "PCSK9",   l2g = 0.6,   pvalue = 1e-8,   vtype = c("intergenic_variant", "intron_variant") )  # Display the results as a tibble for easier analysis l2g_results %>% as_tibble()  # A tibble: 40 × 39    yProbaModel yProbaDistance yProbaInteraction yProbaMolecularQTL yProbaPathogenicity     pval beta.direction beta.betaCI          <dbl>          <dbl>             <dbl>              <dbl>               <dbl>    <dbl> <chr>                <dbl>  1       0.618          0.544             0.056              0.221               0.615 4.79e-12 +                   0.394   2       0.631          0.761             0.284              0.155               0.058 5.23e-20 -                  -0.0687  3       0.631          0.757             0.284              0.155               0.058 3.54e-20 -                  -0.0668  4       0.647          0.739             0.25               0.138               0.182 3   e-47 -                  -0.0544  5       0.652          0.614             0.171              0.637               0.344 3.60e-14 +                   0.0156  6       0.656          0.765             0.284              0.279               0.058 1.13e-41 -                  -0.069   7       0.656          0.765             0.284              0.279               0.058 2.38e-53 -                  -0.0831  8       0.66           0.69              0.171              0.487               0.047 1   e- 8 -                  NA       9       0.663          0.756             0.206              0.33                0.056 1   e-10 -                  -0.03   10       0.664          0.767             0.206              0.614               0.057 1.90e-88 -                  -0.0487 # ℹ 30 more rows # ℹ 31 more variables: beta.betaCILower <dbl>, beta.betaCIUpper <dbl>, odds.oddsCI <dbl>, odds.oddsCILower <dbl>, #   odds.oddsCIUpper <dbl>, study.studyId <chr>, study.traitReported <chr>, study.traitCategory <chr>, study.pubDate <chr>, #   study.pubTitle <chr>, study.pubAuthor <chr>, study.pubJournal <chr>, study.pmid <chr>, study.hasSumstats <lgl>, #   study.nCases <int>, study.numAssocLoci <int>, study.nTotal <int>, study.traitEfos <chr>, variant.id <chr>, variant.rsId <chr>, #   variant.chromosome <chr>, variant.position <int>, variant.refAllele <chr>, variant.altAllele <chr>, #   variant.nearestCodingGeneDistance <int>, variant.nearestGeneDistance <int>, variant.mostSevereConsequence <chr>, …

title: Examples
text: Objective: 🕵️‍ Explore colocalization data specified gene colocalisationsForGene(). Peek genetic overlap may influence multiple traits within genomic neighborhood. Function: colocalisationsForGene() genes: vector intriguing gene identifiers.
code: library(otargen)   # Specify the genes of interest genes_of_interest <- c("ENSG00000163946", "ENSG00000169174", "ENSG00000143001")  # Retrieve colocalization data coloc_data <- colocalisationsForGene(genes = genes_of_interest)  head(coloc_data)  # A tibble: 668 × 20    Study    Trait_reported Lead_variant Molecular_trait Gene_symbol Tissue Source    H3    H4   `log2(H4/H3)`    <chr>    <chr>          <chr>        <chr>           <chr>       <chr>  <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>         <dbl>  1 GCST900… Mean platelet… 3_56619974_… TASOR           ENSG000001… blood  Lepik…     0     1         17.3   2 GCST900… Direct low de… 1_55029009_… PCSK9           ENSG000001… iPSC   PhLiPS     0     1         11.4   3 GCST900… Direct low de… 1_55029009_… PCSK9           ENSG000001… iPSC   iPSCO…     0     1         11.3   4 GCST900… Direct low de… 1_55029009_… PCSK9           ENSG000001… iPSC   HipSci     0     1         11.3   5 GCST900… High choleste… 1_55025188_… PCSK9           ENSG000001… tibia… GTEx-…     0     1         10.7   6 NEALE2_… Yes, because … 1_55026242_… PCSK9           ENSG000001… tibia… GTEx-…     0     1         10.4   7 SAIGE_2… Hyperlipidemia 1_55023869_… PCSK9           ENSG000001… lung   GTEx-…     0     1          9.82  8 SAIGE_2… Hypercholeste… 1_55023869_… PCSK9           ENSG000001… lung   GTEx-…     0     1          9.79  9 SAIGE_2… Disorders of … 1_55023869_… PCSK9           ENSG000001… lung   GTEx-…     0     1          9.77 10 SAIGE_2… Hyperlipidemia 1_55023869_… PCSK9           ENSG000001… tibia… GTEx-…     0     1          9.77 # ℹ 658 more rows # ℹ 10 more variables: Title <chr>, Author <chr>, Has_sumstats <lgl>, numAssocLoci <dbl>, #   `nInitial cohort` <dbl>, study_nReplication <dbl>, study_nCases <dbl>, Publication_date <chr>, #   Journal <chr>, Pubmed_id <chr>

what: Example 4: Quest for QTL Credible Set Data
title: Examples
text: Objective: 🔍 Delve QTLs data qtlCredibleSet(). Unravel tag variants connection traits, one credible set time. Function: qtlCredibleSet() study_id, variant_id, gene, biofeature: Keys unlock treasure trove QTL data.
code: # Retrieve QTL credible set data qtl_cred_set <- qtlCredibleSet(studyid = "Braineac2", variantid = "rs7552841",                               gene = "PCSK9", biofeature = "SUBSTANTIA_NIGRA")  # Display the first few rows of the QTL credible set data head(qtl_cred_set)      tagVariant.id tagVariant.rsId        pval       se      beta    postProb MultisignalMethod   logABF is95 is99 1 1_55052794_A_G              NA 0.000151568 0.166373 -0.681090 0.002299181       conditional 2.726953 TRUE TRUE 2 1_55054539_G_A              NA 0.000482125 0.193346  0.721143 0.001422319       conditional 2.246689 TRUE TRUE 3 1_55241800_A_G              NA 0.000634235 0.181505  0.660898 0.001326061       conditional 2.176614 TRUE TRUE 4 1_55246294_A_G              NA 0.000527554 0.181065  0.670092 0.001424974       conditional 2.248554 TRUE TRUE 5 1_55248288_C_T              NA 0.000650165 0.182720  0.663849 0.001293980       conditional 2.152124 TRUE TRUE 6 1_55248542_G_A              NA 0.000650880 0.182789  0.664035 0.001293430       conditional 2.151698 TRUE TRUE

Phenome-Wide Association Study (PheWAS) analyzes association genetic variants wide range phenotypic traits. retrieve PheWAS analysis results variant, can use pheWAS() function otargen returns tidy data table calculated statistics association specified variant across many traits UK Biobank, FinnGen, ’ GWAS Catalog. Now, shown example snippet piping, output pheWAS() plot_phewas() return nice scatter plot useful color codes interpret associations! even fun, plot allows choose interested keep disease associated variants plot.

what: Example 7: Navigating Genetic Insights with Radar Plots
title: Examples
text: Objective: 🌐 Use radar plot navigational compass interpret complex output studiesAndLeadVariantsForGeneByL2G() plot_l2g(). visualization helps chart course partial scores L2G model prioritizing gene-trait associations like waypoints radar screen guiding exploration. Function: studiesAndLeadVariantsForGeneByL2G() followed plot_l2g() List gene: Identifiers genes interest. disease (optional): specified, focuses analysis single disease. Leave parameter empty wish disease-agnostic prioritize diseases gene based L2G model score associations. top_n_disease (default: 1): Determines number top diseases plot gene, ranked L2G score. Increase value include diseases analysis. Use conjunction disease-agnostic dynamically select top diseases gene. l2g_cutoff (default: 0.5): Sets minimum L2G score threshold diseases considered plot. Increasing value can help reduce plot clutter focusing higher-confidence associations.
code: # Retrieve L2G model statistics for a list of genes  genes = list("ENSG00000167207","ENSG00000096968","ENSG00000138821", "ENSG00000125255") l2g_results <- studiesAndLeadVariantsForGeneByL2G(gene = genes) # Generate radar plot of L2G scores for Iflammatory bowel disease with EFO ID: "EFO_0003767" plot_l2g(l2g_results, disease = "EFO_0003767") # Generate radar plot of L2G scores (l2g score >0.8), without disease parameters specified.  plot_l2g(l2g_results,l2g_cutoff = 0.8)

what: Streamlined Tidy Data Access to Open Target Genetics GraphQL API🔍
title: Access Open Target Genetics
text: otargen open-source R package easy data retrieval analysis Open Target Genetics. brings simplicity scale analsye human genetic evidances gene-trait/diseases associations R environment, enhancing research advanced data handling visualization tools. 📢 otargenpy available python users 🐍 pip install otargenpy Check repository : otargenpy GitHub. otargenpy capabilities otargen, encourage try provide us feedback!

what: Retrieve ChEMBL data for a specified gene and disease. — chemblQuery
title: Retrieve ChEMBL data for a specified gene and disease. — chemblQuery
text: function queries Open Targets Genetics GraphQL API retrieve ChEMBL data specified gene disease, including evidence ChEMBL datasource.

title: Retrieve ChEMBL data for a specified gene and disease. — chemblQuery
Retrieve ChEMBL data for a specified gene and disease. — chemblQuery
text: ensemblId Character: ENSEMBL ID target gene (e.g., ENSG00000169174). efoId Character: EFO ID disease (e.g., EFO_0004911). size Integer: Number records retrieve (default: 10). cursor Character: Cursor pagination (default: NULL).

title: Retrieve ChEMBL data for a specified gene and disease. — chemblQuery
text: Returns data frame containing ChEMBL data specified gene disease.

title: Retrieve ChEMBL data for a specified gene and disease. — chemblQuery
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- chemblQuery(ensemblId = "ENSG00000169174",  efoId = "EFO_0004911", size = 10) result <- chemblQuery(ensemblId = "ENSG00000169174",  efoId = "EFO_0004911", size = 10, cursor = NULL) } # }

what: Retrieves Colocalisation Statistics for a Given Gene — colocalisationsForGene
title: Retrieves Colocalisation Statistics for a Given Gene — colocalisationsForGene
text: Retrieves Colocalisation statistics gene using ENSEMBL gene IDs gene symbol. Colocalisation analysis performed studies Portal least one overlapping associated locus. analysis tests whether two independent associations locus consistent shared causal variant. function supports multiple gene IDs list. returned data frame (tibble format) includes studies evidence colocalisation molecular QTLs selected gene(s).

title: Retrieves Colocalisation Statistics for a Given Gene — colocalisationsForGene
Retrieves Colocalisation Statistics for a Given Gene — colocalisationsForGene
text: genes Character: Gene ENSEMBL ID (e.g. ENSG00000169174) gene symbol (e.g. PCSK9). Multiple gene IDs supported character vector.

title: Retrieves Colocalisation Statistics for a Given Gene — colocalisationsForGene
text: data frame (tibble format) including colocalisation data query gene(s). output tibble contains following columns: Study: Character vector. Study identifier. Trait_reported: Character vector. Reported trait associated colocalisation. Lead_variant: Character vector. Lead variant associated colocalisation. Molecular_trait: Character vector. Molecular trait associated colocalisation. Gene_symbol: Character vector. Gene symbol associated colocalisation. Tissue: Character vector. Tissue colocalisation occurs. Source: Character vector. Source colocalisation data. H3: Numeric vector. H3 value associated colocalisation. log2_H4_H3: Numeric vector. Log2 ratio H4 H3 values. Title: Character vector. Title study. Author: Character vector. Author(s) study. Has_sumstats: Logical vector. Indicates study summary statistics. numAssocLoci: Numeric vector. Number associated loci study. nInitial_cohort: Numeric vector. Number samples initial cohort. study_nReplication: Numeric vector. Number samples replication cohort. study_nCases: Numeric vector. Number cases study. Publication_date: Character vector. Publication date study. Journal: Character vector. Journal study published. Pubmed_id: Character vector. PubMed identifier study.

title: Retrieves Colocalisation Statistics for a Given Gene — colocalisationsForGene
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result1 <- colocalisationsForGene(gene = c("ENSG00000163946", "ENSG00000169174", "ENSG00000143001")) result2 <- colocalisationsForGene(gene = "ENSG00000169174") result3 <- colocalisationsForGene(gene = c("TP53", "TASOR")) result4 <- colocalisationsForGene(gene = "TP53") } # }

what: Retrieves variant-surrounding genes with calculated statistics for causal gene prioritization. — genesForVariant
title: Retrieves variant-surrounding genes with calculated statistics for causal gene prioritization. — genesForVariant
text: function retrieves calculated prioritizing scores surrounding genes specific variant based Open Target Genetics locus--gene (L2G) ML scoring pipeline. provides detailed insights relationship genetic variants genes, allowing users explore impact variants gene expression, colocalization scores, chromatin interactions, predicted functional effects. function returns information structured format, making easier analyze interpret results.

title: Retrieves variant-surrounding genes with calculated statistics for causal gene prioritization. — genesForVariant
Retrieves variant-surrounding genes with calculated statistics for causal gene prioritization. — genesForVariant
text: variant_id character string specifying ID variant fetch gene information.

title: Retrieves variant-surrounding genes with calculated statistics for causal gene prioritization. — genesForVariant
text: list following components: v2g: data frame variant--gene information following data structure:     - gene.symbol: character     - variant: character     - overallScore: numeric     - gene.id: character tssd: data frame details colocalization scores effect variant expression genes across tissues following data structure:     - gene.symbol: character     - variant: character     - typeId: character     - sourceId: character     - aggregatedScore: numeric     - tissues_distance: integer     - tissues_score: numeric     - tissues_quantile: numeric     - tissues_id: character     - tissues_name: character qtls: List QTL associations genes variants across analyzed tissues following data structure:     - gene.symbol: character     - variant: character     - typeId: character     - aggregatedScore: numeric     - tissues_quantile: numeric     - tissues_beta: numeric     - tissues_pval: numeric     - tissues_id: character     - tissues_name: character chromatin: data frame including information chromatin interactions effect involving genes variants following data structure:     - gene.symbol: character     - variant: character     - typeId: character     - sourceId: character     - aggregatedScore: numeric     - tissues_quantile: numeric     - tissues_score: numeric     - tissues_id: character     - tissues_name: character functionalpred: data frame including predicted functional effects variants genes across tissues following data structure:     - gene.symbol: character     - variant: character     - typeId: character     - sourceId: character     - aggregatedScore: numeric     - tissues_maxEffectLabel: character     - tissues_maxEffectScore: numeric     - tissues_id: character     - tissues_name: character

title: Retrieves variant-surrounding genes with calculated statistics for causal gene prioritization. — genesForVariant

what: Retrieve summary information of the genes in a locus — getLociGenes
title: Retrieve summary information of the genes in a locus — getLociGenes
text: function retrieves information specified region chromosome, including overlapping genes associated details ID, symbol, biotype, description,  transcription start site (TSS), start position, end position, strand direction, exons structure.

title: Retrieve summary information of the genes in a locus — getLociGenes
Retrieve summary information of the genes in a locus — getLociGenes
text: chromosome Character: Chromosome number string. start Integer: Start position specified region chromosome. end Integer: End position specified region chromosome.

title: Retrieve summary information of the genes in a locus — getLociGenes
text: Returns tibble data frame overlapping genes specified region following data structure: id: Character. ID gene. symbol: Character. Symbol gene. bioType: Character. Biotype gene. description: Character. Description gene. chromosome: Character. Chromosome gene. tss: Integer. Transcription start site gene. start: Integer. Start position gene. end: Integer. End position gene. fwdStrand: Logical. Strand direction gene. exons: List. List exons gene.

title: Retrieve summary information of the genes in a locus — getLociGenes

what: Retrieve calculated GWAS colocalisation data — gwasColocalisation
title: Retrieve calculated GWAS colocalisation data — gwasColocalisation
text: function retrieves colocalisation data specific variant study GWAS studies. returns data frame studies colocalise input variant study, including details study reported trait, index variant, calculated coloc method (see Ref. ) outputs.

title: Retrieve calculated GWAS colocalisation data — gwasColocalisation
Retrieve calculated GWAS colocalisation data — gwasColocalisation
text: study_id Character: Open Target Genetics generated ID GWAS study. variant_id Character: Open Target Genetics generated ID variant (CHRPOSITION_REFALLELE_ALTALLELE rsID).

title: Retrieve calculated GWAS colocalisation data — gwasColocalisation
text: Returns data frame studies colocalise input variant study. table consists following data structure: study.studyId: Character vector. Study identifier. study.traitReported: Character vector. Reported trait associated colocalisation. study.traitCategory: Character vector. Trait category. indexVariant.id: Character vector. Index variant identifier. indexVariant.position: Integer vector. Index variant position. indexVariant.chromosome: Character vector. Index variant chromosome. indexVariant.rsId: Character vector. Index variant rsID. beta: Numeric vector. Beta value associated colocalisation. h3: Numeric vector. H3 value associated colocalisation. h4: Numeric vector. H4 value associated colocalisation. log2h4h3: Numeric vector. Log2 ratio H4 H3 values.

title: Retrieve calculated GWAS colocalisation data — gwasColocalisation
text: Giambartolomei, Claudia et al. “Bayesian test colocalisation pairs genetic association studies using summary statistics.” PLoS genetics vol. 10,5 e1004383. 15 May. 2014, doi:10.1371/journal.pgen.1004383

title: Retrieve calculated GWAS colocalisation data — gwasColocalisation
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ colocalisation_data <- gwasColocalisation(study_id = "GCST90002357", variant_id = "1_154119580_C_A") colocalisation_data <- gwasColocalisation(study_id = "GCST90002357", variant_id = "rs2494663") } # }

what: Retrieve GWAS colocalisation data for a genomic region . — gwasColocalisationForRegion
title: Retrieve GWAS colocalisation data for a genomic region . — gwasColocalisationForRegion

what: Retrieve GWAS summary statistics to create a Manhattan plot — manhattan
title: Retrieve GWAS summary statistics to create a Manhattan plot — manhattan
text: function retrieves summary statistics given GWAS study ID, used generate Manhattan plot. Manhattan plot graphical representation genetic association studies, particularly genome-wide association studies (GWAS). displays results statistical associations genetic variants trait disease interest across genome. function returns data frame underlying data, can used recreate Manhattan plot using plot_manhattan() function, custom plots downstream analysis.

title: Retrieve GWAS summary statistics to create a Manhattan plot — manhattan
Retrieve GWAS summary statistics to create a Manhattan plot — manhattan
text: study_id Character: Open Targets Genetics generated ID GWAS study. pageindex Int: Index current page (pagination index >= 0). pagesize Int: Number records page (pagination size > 0).

title: Retrieve GWAS summary statistics to create a Manhattan plot — manhattan
text: Returns data frame containing Manhattan associations input study ID. table consists following columns: pval_mantissa: Numeric vector. Mantissa p-value. pval_exponent: Integer vector. Exponent p-value. credible_set_size: Integer vector. Size credible set. ld_set_size: Integer vector. Size LD set. total_set_size: Integer vector. Total size set. pval: Numeric vector. P-value. odds_ratio: Logical vector. Odds ratio. odds_ratio_ci_lower: Logical vector. Lower confidence interval odds ratio. odds_ratio_ci_upper: Logical vector. Upper confidence interval odds ratio. beta: Numeric vector. Beta value. beta_ci_lower: Numeric vector. Lower confidence interval beta value. beta_ci_upper: Numeric vector. Upper confidence interval beta value. direction: Character vector. Direction effect. best_genes_score: Numeric vector. Score best genes. best_genes_gene_id: Character vector. Gene ID best genes. best_genes_gene_symbol: Character vector. Gene symbol best genes. best_coloc_genes_score: Numeric vector. Score best colocated genes. best_coloc_genes_gene_id: Character vector. Gene ID best colocated genes. best_coloc_genes_gene_symbol: Character vector. Gene symbol best colocated genes. best_locus2genes_score: Numeric vector. Score best locus--genes. best_locus2genes_gene_id: Character vector. Gene ID best locus--genes. best_locus2genes_gene_symbol: Character vector. Gene symbol best locus--genes. variant_id: Character vector. Variant ID. variant_position: Integer vector. Variant position. variant_chromosome: Character vector. Variant chromosome. variant_rs_id: Character vector. Variant rsID.

title: Retrieve GWAS summary statistics to create a Manhattan plot — manhattan
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- manhattan(study_id = "GCST90002357") result <- manhattan(study_id = "GCST90002357", pageindex = 2, pagesize = 50) } # }

what: Retrieve PheWAS (Phenome Wide Association Studies) data for a variant. — pheWAS
title: Retrieve PheWAS (Phenome Wide Association Studies) data for a variant. — pheWAS
text: PheWAS (Phenome-wide association study) method investigates relationships genetic variants traits phenotypes, helping study potential influence multiple traits diseases concurrently. function retrieves traits associated given variant UK Biobank, FinnGen, /GWAS Catalog summary statistics repository (traits p-value less 0.005 returned).

title: Retrieve PheWAS (Phenome Wide Association Studies) data for a variant. — pheWAS
Retrieve PheWAS (Phenome Wide Association Studies) data for a variant. — pheWAS

title: Retrieve PheWAS (Phenome Wide Association Studies) data for a variant. — pheWAS
text: data frame PheWAS associations. output data frame contains following columns: totalGWASStudies: integer indicating total number GWAS studies variant associated. pval: numeric value representing p-value association variant trait. beta: numeric value representing beta value, represents effect size variant trait. oddsRatio: numeric value representing odds ratio, measuring association variant trait. nTotal: integer indicating total number participants study. study.studyId: character vector representing study ID. study.source: character vector representing source study. study.pmid: character vector representing PubMed ID (PMID) study. study.pubDate: character vector representing publication date study. study.traitReported: character vector representing reported trait associated variant. study.traitCategory: character vector representing category trait.

Plot PheWAS() results. — plot_phewas
text: plot visualizes traits associated user's selected variant UK Biobank, FinnGen, /GWAS Catalog summary statistics repository based PheWAS analysis. associated traits mapped onto x-axis, corresponding -log10(p-value) values plotted y-axis. horizontal line shown p-value cutoff 0.005 indicate significant associations. Associations cutoff labeled trait's name, sources associations color-coded points.

what: Retrieve QTL colocalisation results of a variant — qtlColocalisationVariantQuery
title: Retrieve QTL colocalisation results of a variant — qtlColocalisationVariantQuery
text: colocalisation analysis Open Target Genetics performed using coloc method (Giambartolomei et al., 2014).   Coloc Bayesian method , two traits, integrates evidence variants   locus evaluate following hypotheses:   - H0: association either trait   - H1: Association trait 1, trait 2   - H2: Association trait 2, trait 1   - H3: Association trait 1 trait 2, two independent SNPs   - H4: Association trait 1 trait 2, one shared SNP   analysis tests whether two independent associations locus consistent   shared causal variant. Colocalisation two independent associations two GWAS studies may suggest shared causal mechanism.

Beta value. h4: Numeric. h4 value. h3: Numeric. h3 value. log2h4h3: Numeric. Log2(h4/h3) value.

Retrieve
text: "locus--gene" (L2G) model derives features prioritize likely causal genes GWAS locus based genetic functional genomics features. main categories predictive features : Distance: Distance credible set variants gene. Molecular QTL colocalization: Colocalization molecular QTLs. Chromatin interaction: Interactions, promoter-capture Hi-C. Variant pathogenicity: Pathogenicity scores VEP (Variant Effect Predictor).

title: Retrieve
code: studiesAndLeadVariantsForGeneByL2G(gene, l2g = NA, pvalue = NA, vtype = NULL)

title: Retrieve
text: gene Character: Gene ENSEMBL ID (e.g. ENSG00000169174) gene symbol (e.g. PCSK9). argument can take list genes . l2g Numeric: Locus--gene (L2G) cutoff score. (Default: NA) pvalue Character: P-value cutoff. (Default: NA) vtype Character: severe consequence filter variant types, including "intergenic_variant", "upstream_gene_variant", "intron_variant", "missense_variant", "5_prime_UTR_variant", "non_coding_transcript_exon_variant", "splice_region_variant". (Default: NULL)

title: Retrieve
text: Returns data frame containing input gene ID data L2G model. table consists following columns: yProbaModel: Numeric. L2G score. yProbaDistance: Numeric. Distance. yProbaInteraction: Numeric. Chromatin interaction. yProbaMolecularQTL: Numeric. Molecular QTL. yProbaPathogenicity: Numeric. Pathogenicity. pval: Numeric. P-value. beta.direction: Character. Beta direction. beta.betaCI: Numeric. Beta confidence interval. beta.betaCILower: Numeric. Lower bound beta confidence interval. beta.betaCIUpper: Numeric. Upper bound beta confidence interval. odds.oddsCI: Numeric. Odds ratio confidence interval. odds.oddsCILower: Numeric. Lower bound odds ratio confidence interval. odds.oddsCIUpper: Numeric. Upper bound odds ratio confidence interval. study.studyId: Character. Study ID. study.traitReported: Character. Reported trait. study.traitCategory: Character. Trait category. study.pubDate: Character. Publication date. study.pubTitle: Character. Publication title. study.pubAuthor: Character. Publication author. study.pubJournal: Character. Publication journal. study.pmid: Character. PubMed ID. study.hasSumstats: Logical. Indicates study summary statistics. study.nCases: Integer. Number cases study. study.numAssocLoci: Integer. Number associated loci. study.nTotal: Integer. Total number samples study. study.traitEfos: Character. Trait EFOs. variant.id: Character. Variant ID. variant.rsId: Character. Variant rsID. variant.chromosome: Character. Variant chromosome. variant.position: Integer. Variant position. variant.refAllele: Character. Variant reference allele. variant.altAllele: Character. Variant alternate allele. variant.nearestCodingGeneDistance: Integer. Distance nearest coding gene. variant.nearestGeneDistance: Integer. Distance nearest gene. variant.mostSevereConsequence: Character. severe consequence. variant.nearestGene.id: Character. Nearest gene ID. variant.nearestCodingGene.id: Character. Nearest coding gene ID. ensembl_id: Character. Ensembl ID. gene_symbol: Character. Gene symbol.

title: Retrieve

text: study_id Character: Study ID(s) generated Open Targets Genetics (e.g GCST90002357). variant_id Character: generated ID variants Open Targets Genetics (e.g. 1_154119580_C_A) rsId (rs2494663).

title: Retrieve the locus-to-gene (L2G) data table for loci genes. — studyLocus2GeneTable
text: Returns data frame summary statistics study data table containing various calculated scores features lead variant. output table following data structure: studyId: Character. Study ID. variant.id: Character. Variant ID. variant.rsId: Character. Variant rsID. yProbaDistance: Numeric. Distance score. yProbaModel: Numeric. Model score. yProbaMolecularQTL: Numeric. Molecular QTL score. yProbaPathogenicity: Numeric. Pathogenicity score. yProbaInteraction: Numeric. Interaction score. hasColoc: Logical. Indicates colocalization data available. distanceToLocus: Numeric. Distance locus. gene.id: Character. Gene ID. gene.symbol: Character. Gene symbol.

title: Retrieve the locus-to-gene (L2G) data table for loci genes. — studyLocus2GeneTable
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- studyLocus2GeneTable(study_id = "GCST90002357", variant_id = "1_154119580_C_A") result <- studyLocus2GeneTable(study_id = "GCST90002357", variant_id = "rs2494663") } # }

what: Retrieves all variants for a study. — studyVariants
title: Retrieves all variants for a study. — studyVariants
text: input study ID, function returns information variants across associated loci. output also includes  information associated genes within loci.

title: Retrieves all variants for a study. — studyVariants
Retrieves all variants for a study. — studyVariants
text: study_id Character: Study ID(s) generated Open Targets Genetics (e.g GCST90002357).

title: Retrieves all variants for a study. — studyVariants
text: Returns list two data frames. first data frame (tibble format) includes loci data frame following data structure: variant.id: Character. Variant ID. pval: Numeric. P-value. variant.nearestCodingGene.symbol: Character. Symbol nearest coding gene variant. variant.rsId: Character. Variant rsID. variant.chromosome: Character. Chromosome variant. variant.position: Integer. Position variant. variant.nearestCodingGeneDistance: Integer. Distance nearest coding gene. credibleSetSize: Integer. Size credible set. ldSetSize: Integer. Size LD set. oddsRatio: Numeric. Odds ratio. beta: Numeric. Beta value. second data frame includes gene information following data structure: score: Numeric. Gene score. gene.id: Character. Gene ID. gene.symbol: Character. Gene symbol.

title: Retrieves all variants for a study. — studyVariants

title: Retrieves tag variants and studies for a given index variant. — tagVariantsAndStudiesForIndexVariant
Retrieves tag variants and studies for a given index variant. — tagVariantsAndStudiesForIndexVariant
text: variant_id Character: Open Targets Genetics generated ID variant (CHRPOSITION_REFALLELE_ALTALLELE rsId). pageindex Integer: Index current page pagination (>= 0). pagesize Integer: Number records page pagination (> 0).

title: Retrieves tag variants and studies for a given index variant. — tagVariantsAndStudiesForIndexVariant
text: Returns data frame containing variant associations connected input index variant. columns data frame follows: tagVariant.id: Character. Tag variant ID. tagVariant.chromosome: Character. Chromosome tag variant. tagVariant.rsId: Character. rsID tag variant. tagVariant.position: Integer. Position tag variant. study.studyId: Character. Study ID. study.traitReported: Character. Reported trait study. study.traitCategory: Character. Category trait study. pval: Numeric. P-value. pvalMantissa: Numeric. Mantissa p-value. pvalExponent: Integer. Exponent p-value. nTotal: Integer. Total number samples. nCases: Integer. Number cases study. overallR2: Numeric. Overall R-squared value. afr1000GProp: Numeric. Proportion African 1000 Genomes population. amr1000GProp: Numeric. Proportion Admixed American 1000 Genomes population. eas1000GProp: Numeric. Proportion East Asian 1000 Genomes population. eur1000GProp: Numeric. Proportion European 1000 Genomes population. sas1000GProp: Numeric. Proportion South Asian 1000 Genomes population. oddsRatio: Numeric. Odds ratio. oddsRatioCILower: Numeric. Lower bound odds ratio confidence interval. oddsRatioCIUpper: Numeric. Upper bound odds ratio confidence interval. posteriorProbability: Numeric. Posterior probability. beta: Numeric. Beta value. betaCILower: Numeric. Lower bound beta value confidence interval. betaCIUpper: Numeric. Upper bound beta value confidence interval. direction: Character. Direction effect. log10Abf: Numeric. Log base 10 approximate Bayes factor.

title: Retrieves tag variants and studies for a given index variant. — tagVariantsAndStudiesForIndexVariant

Reported trait input study. study.traitCategory: Character. Category trait input study. topStudiesByLociOverlap.studyId: Character. what: Retrieves information about a variant. — variantInfo
title: Retrieves information about a variant. — variantInfo
text: given variant ID, function retrieves information variant, including chromosome, position, reference allele, alternative allele, rsID, nearest gene, severe consequence, allele frequencies different populations gnomAD databse. Genome Aggregation Database (gnomAD) resource developed international coalition investigators, goal aggregating harmonizing exome genome sequencing data wide variety large-scale sequencing projects, making summary data available wider scientific community (see reference).

title: Retrieves information about a variant. — variantInfo
Retrieves information about a variant. — variantInfo
text: variant_id Character: generated ID variants Open Targets Genetics (e.g. 1_154119580_C_A) rsId (rs2494663).

title: Retrieves information about a variant. — variantInfo
text: Returns data frame (tibble format) containing information variant. data frame following structure: chromosome: Character. Chromosome variant. position: Integer. Position variant. refAllele: Character. Reference allele. altAllele: Character. Alternative allele. rsId: Character. Variant rsID. chromosomeB37: Character. Chromosome variant build 37 coordinates. positionB37: Integer. Position variant build 37 coordinates. id: Character. Variant ID. nearestGene.id: Character. ID nearest gene variant. nearestGene.symbol: Character. Symbol nearest gene variant. nearestGeneDistance: Integer. Distance nearest gene. nearestCodingGene.id: Character. ID nearest coding gene variant. nearestCodingGene.symbol: Character. Symbol nearest coding gene variant. nearestCodingGeneDistance: Integer. Distance nearest coding gene. mostSevereConsequence: Character. severe consequence variant. caddRaw: Numeric. CADD raw score. caddPhred: Numeric. CADD phred score. gnomadAFR: Numeric. Allele frequency African/African-American population gnomAD. gnomadAMR: Numeric. Allele frequency Latino/Admixed American population gnomAD. gnomadASJ: Numeric. Allele frequency Ashkenazi Jewish population gnomAD. gnomadEAS: Numeric. Allele frequency East Asian population gnomAD. gnomadFIN: Numeric. Allele frequency Finnish population gnomAD. gnomadNFE: Numeric. Allele frequency Non-Finnish European population gnomAD. gnomadNFEEST: Numeric. Allele frequency Estonian population gnomAD. gnomadNFENWE: Numeric. Allele frequency Northwest European population gnomAD. gnomadNFESEU: Numeric. Allele frequency Southern European population gnomAD. gnomadNFEONF: Numeric. Allele frequency Non-Finnish European population gnomAD. gnomadOTH: Numeric. Allele frequency populations gnomAD.

what: Added frequently requested query functions from Open Target to the package
title: otargen 1.1.5
text: chemblQuery(): function queries Open Targets GraphQL API retrieve ChEMBL data specified gene disease, including evidence ChEMBL datasource. clinvarQuery(): #’ function queries Open Targets GraphQL API retrieve ClinVar data specified gene disease, including evidence NCBI datasource. knownDrugsQuery(): function queries Open Targets GraphQL API retrieve known drugs data specified gene. function queries Open Targets GraphQL API retrieve mouse phenotypes data specified gene. compGenomicsQuery() function queries Open Targets GraphQL API retrieve comparative genomics data specified gene.

title: otargen 1.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-02-21

title: otargen 1.1.1
text: Fixed several inconsistencies parameters naming functions used examples article main function package. Streamline examples description short organized. Added additional plotting case example 7 additional parameters added current version plot_l2g function.

title: otargen 1.1.1
text: Fixed issue plot_l2g() function cases disease parameter selected. previous function plotting top disease one gene. function now updated two parameters expand plot_l2g() functionality plotting disease agnostic scenario. two parameters : top_n_disease (default: 1): Determines number top diseases plot gene, ranked L2G score. Increase value include diseases analysis. Use conjunction disease-agnostic dynamically select top diseases gene. l2g_cutoff (default: 0.5): Sets minimum L2G score threshold diseases considered plot. Increasing value can help reduce plot clutter focusing higher-confidence associations.

title: otargen 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-12-12

title: otargen 1.1.0
text: Fixed issue variantInfo function wrong variable (result) referenced, leading ‘object found’ error. Modified variantInfo() correctly process API response data. Additionally, added checks ensure results NULL processing, improves function’s robustness error handling.

title: otargen 1.1.0
text: Improved test plot_l2g.R test case provide better error handling diagnostic information. test now uses tryCatch block gracefully handle errors explicitly fail clear message, enhancing reliability maintainability test suite.

title: otargen 1.1.0
text: Fixed issue genesForVariant() giving error using rsId variants.

title: otargen 1.1.0
text: Updated functions include connection timeout checks. Added try-catch blocks handle timeouts API requests gracefully, ensuring functions provide informative error messages fail safely case network connection issues.

title: otargen 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-06-23

title: otargen 1.0.0
text: first release otargen.
